topic: A person finds out their biography was written without their knowledge
raw_output:
[story 1]

The glossy paper felt like sandpaper under my fingertips as I unfolded the document. Every sentence, every carefully chosen anecdote, was a lie woven into the fabric of my existence. My breath hitched, a shallow, ragged sound in the sudden silence of the study. I dropped the paper; it fluttered to the thick rug, a meaningless scrap of deceit. My hands began to tremble uncontrollably, the fine motor control dissolving into a frantic shaking. I backed away from the desk, stumbling against a bookshelf, my vision blurring at the edges. "No," I whispered, the sound thin and reedy. "This isn't me." A hot flush crept up my neck, turning my skin tight and prickly. I needed air, desperately, but the air felt thick, suffocating. My chest constricted, a painful vise squeezing the breath from my lungs. I pressed the heels of my hands against my eyes, trying to force the images away, but the words swam, mocking me from the periphery of my sight.

[story 2]

It started as a misplaced footnote, a single, glaring discrepancy in my childhood address. Then the whole edifice crumbled. I read the curated narrative of my life—the triumphs, the tragedies, the very contours of my personality—and realized it was all someone else's script. A low, involuntary moan escaped my throat, a sound utterly foreign to my own ears. I started pacing the length of the room, my movements jerky and uncoordinated. My jaw clenched so tightly I could feel the muscles strain beneath my skin. I stopped abruptly, spinning around, my eyes wide and unfocused, darting around the room as if expecting the author to materialize from the shadows. "Impossible," I managed, the word cracking on the edge. My hands flew up, gripping the arms of a nearby chair until my knuckles were white and aching. I began to rub my temples vigorously, a desperate attempt to physically scrub the impossible reality from my mind. The room seemed to tilt; the colors sharpened too intensely, and a strange, buzzing energy thrummed just beneath my skin, making my skin crawl.

[story 3]

The realization hit me like a physical blow, stealing the air from my lungs entirely. I stared at the printed pages, the ink seeming to writhe before my gaze. A strange, high-pitched tremor started in my hands, escalating rapidly until I had to clamp them over my mouth to stop the involuntary sounds escaping. I felt a profound disconnect, as if I were watching a poorly acted play of my own life. I began to shake, a full-body vibration that started in my core and radiated outward, making my knees buckle slightly. I tried to speak, to articulate the sheer absurdity of it all, but only a choked gasp emerged. I clawed at my own arms, feeling a desperate need to tear something apart, anything solid, to anchor myself to reality. My vision tunneled, the edges darkening into a swirling grey. I sank to my knees, the floor rushing up to meet me, my whole body vibrating with a panicked, uncontrolled energy that felt utterly overwhelming.
